title: Should I even bother with Rebuild
display_title: Should I even bother with Rebuild
wikitext:
[]: Should I even bother with Rebuild?             Quote Post by Rahaquiel-2D1S » Fri Nov 20, 2009 5:56 pm Is it possible to watch the Rebuild films as a stand-alone movie series, or does it rely on Wild Mass Guessing (You'd know what I mean if you go to TV Tropes)? Like, is it possible to understand it or do you just have to make something up?

[]:             Quote Post by Great Genius Shinji-Sama » Fri Nov 20, 2009 6:09 pm IMO, you dont need to make stuff up. THey changed things a LITTLE here and there; but nothing to make you "unlearn what you have learned" So to speak.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Fri Nov 20, 2009 6:26 pm It's a different continuation so yeah, it's possible.

[]: Re: Should I even bother with Rebuild?             Quote Post by esselfortium » Fri Nov 20, 2009 6:55 pm Rahaquiel-2D1S wrote: Should I even bother with Rebuild?

[]:             Quote Post by Dr. Webster » Fri Nov 20, 2009 7:10 pm I'm hesitant about Rebuild 2.0 and onward because of the changes in the storyline -- I'm very much a fan of the way the original episodes played out -- but in the very least I'd say it's worth it to watch Rebuild 1.0. The changes they made in Rebuild 1.0 compared to the first six TV episodes seem to help new viewers understand what's going on a little better. For me, the first time I watched the TV series it seemed like the first half of it was normal mecha fare, then it started getting very weird very quickly. Rebuild 1.0 seems to set the stage upfront that this ain't your typical giant robot anime.

[]: Re: Should I even bother with Rebuild?             Quote Post by Xard » Fri Nov 20, 2009 7:18 pm Rahaquiel-2D1S wrote: Is it possible to watch the Rebuild films as a stand-alone movie series, or does it rely on Wild Mass Guessing (You'd know what I mean if you go to TV Tropes)? Like, is it possible to understand it or do you just have to make something up?

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Fri Nov 20, 2009 7:18 pm Dr. Webster wrote: I'm hesitant about Rebuild 2.0 and onward because of the changes in the storyline -- I'm very much a fan of the way the original episodes played out -- but in the very least I'd say it's worth it to watch Rebuild 1.0. The changes they made in Rebuild 1.0 compared to the first six TV episodes seem to help new viewers understand what's going on a little better. For me, the first time I watched the TV series it seemed like the first half of it was normal mecha fare, then it started getting very weird very quickly. Rebuild 1.0 seems to set the stage upfront that this ain't your typical giant robot anime.

[]:             Quote Post by backseatjesus » Fri Nov 20, 2009 7:21 pm Thank you for making another thread for people to blowup in. Decide for yourself.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Fri Nov 20, 2009 7:22 pm backseatjesus wrote: Thank you for making another thread for people to blowup in. Decide for yourself.

[]:             Quote Post by backseatjesus » Fri Nov 20, 2009 7:25 pm Xard wrote: backseatjesus wrote: Thank you for making another thread for people to blowup in. Decide for yourself.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Nov 20, 2009 7:26 pm Xard: But the bitching is pointless in general. Anyway, decide for yourself whether or not you want to see the films. Personally, I feel 1.0 and 2.0 are 10/10, but I am planning on watching all four films back to back once they're all released.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Fri Nov 20, 2009 7:28 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: Xard: But the bitching is pointless in general.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Fri Nov 20, 2009 7:33 pm Xard: But the bitching is pointless in general.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Fri Nov 20, 2009 7:36 pm Joseph the PRPD wrote: Isn't Xard drunk?

[]: Re: Should I even bother with Rebuild?             Quote Post by Rock-Slash » Fri Nov 20, 2009 8:30 pm Rahaquiel-2D1S wrote: Should I even bother with Rebuild?

[]:             Quote Post by chaosakita » Fri Nov 20, 2009 8:48 pm Lots of people have found it entertaining, and I don't see why you wouldn't either.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Nov 20, 2009 9:09 pm Xard: I was agreeing that the complaining is pointless.

[]:             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Fri Nov 20, 2009 9:27 pm I think its cool to have alternate continuities however, I already know that 2.0 is where the real fun begins

[]: Re: Should I even bother with Rebuild?             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Nov 20, 2009 10:55 pm Rahaquiel-2D1S wrote: Should I even bother with Rebuild?

[]: Re: Should I even bother with Rebuild?             Quote Post by Eva 04 » Sat Nov 21, 2009 1:55 am Rahaquiel-2D1S wrote: Is it possible to watch the Rebuild films as a stand-alone movie series, or does it rely on Wild Mass Guessing (You'd know what I mean if you go to TV Tropes)? Like, is it possible to understand it or do you just have to make something up?
